repo_prior_art.cite: stat_pn_weak_event_honest_inference studies rare factual-event PN/PS ratios, not a CAR-censored illness-death stochastic-intervention EIF.

published_prior_art.cite: MartinussenKnudsenRytgaard2026Debiased supplies fixed-window illness-death EIF inference; SchindlShenKennedy2024IncrementalContinuous supplies static continuous-exposure effective-sample-size results; neither states a shrinking-window CAR multistate marked-compound-Poisson experiment.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_dose_response_minimax and stat_pn_weak_event_honest_inference concern, respectively, static-dose minimax lower bounds and rare PN/PS intervals, not Hölder regularization of an exact initiation-time survival target.

published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2020HonestCI and AiHuangZhang2026UniformContinuousTreatment provide honest/local continuous-treatment inference tools, but neither treats learned CAR illness-death EIFs or the bounded-effective-count replacement experiment.

soundness_flags.label: def:bias-aware-interval and oeq:exact-target-honesty
soundness_flags.one_line: The set \(\mathcal B^G_\alpha\) is defined using \(C^{\mathrm{BA}}_{n,\alpha}(b_n)\), but the displayed interval instead calls \(\widehat\theta_b(0)\) and \(\widehat s_n(0)\) without requiring \(0\in\mathcal C\) or specifying how \(b(0)\) equals the proposed sequence \(b_n\); hence the candidate procedure and its diameter are not defined for a general \((b_n)\).

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:observed-hazard-causal-validity and both open-ended questions
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Correspondence between the observed-filtration hazard intervention and the full-filtration causal intervention is assumed rather than identified from observed trajectories.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: All causal-target and inference claims are conditional on existence of a compatible full-data extension satisfying the stated observed-hazard causal-validity restriction.

source_verification_receipts.locator: Abstract; paper Section 3 and Theorem 3.1 as cited in the core
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The preprint uses an illness-death model, distinguishes an observed-data stochastic initiation-hazard intervention from a causally valid full-data intervention, identifies fixed-time non-pathwise differentiability, and introduces a smoothed intervention with an EIF and debiased one-step estimator.
Theorem 2.1 provides a Gaussian approximation for suprema of empirical processes under moment, entropy, measurability, and variance conditions; it does not supply a rare-event complement experiment.
The paper studies exponentially tilted static continuous-exposure interventions, derives an EIF and efficiency bound, and gives effective-sample-size \(n/\delta\) minimax and estimator-rate results.
The paper constructs bias-aware honest confidence intervals for scalar nonparametric-regression targets and studies kernel choice, efficiency, and bandwidth selection.

The paper develops minimum-variance weighted local-linear continuous-treatment estimation, uniform Bahadur representations, bootstrap bands, undersmoothing, and bias-control bands for a general dose-response function.
The article gives kernel-localized, cross-fitted doubly robust inference for average dose-response functions with high-level nuisance conditions; it is not a multistate CAR survival analysis.

The article derives minimax rates and a local-polynomial estimator for Hölder-smooth CATE estimation, including a localized lower-bound construction; it is a valid analogue for localized Hölder point-target methodology, not a survival result.
The paper studies global and pointwise dose-response estimation, including doubly robust and higher-order estimators with mean-square-error bounds.

The paper gives cross-fitted doubly robust treatment-specific survival-curve inference with pointwise and uniform asymptotic linearity under nuisance conditions, for baseline treatment rather than a shrinking initiation hazard.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define a bandwidth-sequence-specific procedure \(C^{\mathrm{BA}}_{n,\alpha}[b_n]\), or require \(0\in\mathcal C\) and \(b(0)=b_n\); then use that same object in \(\mathcal B^G_\alpha\) and the diameter objective.

State a concrete primitive sufficient condition for the Gaussian branch—localized nuisance-rate products, an inverse-information bound, and an explicit admissible bandwidth range—and make its resulting limit law the first-result target.

Commit the honest-inference branch to an explicit two-point or modulus family in \(\mathcal P_s^0(L)\) and a candidate diameter order such as the balance of \(b^s\) and \((nb)^{-1/2}\), then test whether the CAR score changes that frontier.

recommended_next_step: Repair the bandwidth/procedure typing in the honest-inference definition, then advance the two genuinely new open kernels to Stage 0.
